column3:
Instructions:
1. Place an "X" in the box that maps each CLO to the PLO(s) it aligns with. (It should have moderate to strong alignment)

2. In the far right column, indicate with a "I, R, or M" the level of development of the PLO in this course.

column11: Level of Development (I, R, or M)

column5: Explain key physical, chemical, and biological processes in freshwater systems, including lakes, reservoirs, rivers, and wetlands.
column6: Analyze limnological data, applying quantitative and graphical techniques to assess ecosystem dynamics.
column7: Evaluate human impacts on freshwater ecosystems, such as nutrient enrichment, invasive species, and climate change.
column8: Conduct field and laboratory investigations, demonstrating proper sampling techniques, data collection, and analytical procedures used in limnology.
column9: Integrate limnological principles to propose management or conservation strategies for freshwater resources.
column10: Communicate scientific findings effectively, both orally and in written technical reports.

column3: Demonstrate knowledge of the ethical, economic, and social implications of biological discovery and technological innovation.
Discuss the historical and social context of biological thought and research, including Indigenous ways of knowing.
Use qualitative observation, quantitative reasoning, and analytical skills for scientific problem solving of biological data from wet labs, dry labs, and/or field studies.
Apply scientific inquiry methods and critical thinking skills to investigate and analyze biological problems.

Complete all stages of a full scientific investigation that is transparent, open, and reproducible, including (i) exploratory research informing, (ii) question and hypothesis formulation, (iii) experimental or observational study design, (iv) data collection, (v) data analysis, (vi) data and code archiving, (vii) data presentation and visualization, and (viii) communication and dissemination of all research outputs in accessible and useable formats.
Discriminate among scientific claims by finding relevant research, identifying bias, and critically evaluating data and conclusions.

Effectively communicate scientific ideas and findings through oral presentations, written reports, and digital media using appropriate terminology, data visualization and formatting for diverse audiences including relevant stakeholders.
Develop and defend logical, structured, and coherent claims with appropriate supporting evidence while acknowledging potential bias.
Demonstrate the ability to work independently and collaboratively to solve biological problems, through mutual respect, leadership skills, goal setting, and task and time management.
Demonstrate personal, academic, and professional integrity by respecting diverse perspectives, valuing the intellectual contribution of others, and following ethical standards in scientific practice.
